title: CSS Preprocessors Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating modular, programmatically-advanced CSS using preprocessors.

questions.question: Which of these is the MOST popular and well-supported CSS preprocessor available today?
options: SASS
SCSS
Both 'SASS' and 'SCSS' are synonymous.
None of these.

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using a CSS preprocessor like SASS?
options: It makes your CSS files smaller automatically.
It allows you to use advanced features like Variables, Nesting, and Reusable Mixins.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is the 'Nesting' feature in SASS used for?
options: To write CSS rules inside each other to match the underlying HTML structure.
To hide images from certain types of users.
To define the website's visual theme and fonts.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: What are 'Partials' (modular files) in a preprocessor primarily used for?
options: To create separate images for each CSS file.
To split your large CSS into many small, manageable files that are combined later during build.
To stop the site from loading too quickly.
To hide code from search engines.

questions.question: Which tool uses JavaScript-based plugins to transform your CSS (e.g., automatically adding -webkit- vendor prefixes)?
options: SASS
PostCSS
LESS
Stylus
